summary: Soundboard recording of solid quality captures a landmark 1969 show that swings from laid‑back country (Slewfoot, Dire Wolf) into a soaring Dark Star > St Stephen > The Eleven sequence.
review: The performance is praised for its rare early‑year material—especially the pedal‑steel‑laden Slewfoot and Bobby’s lead on Dire Wolf—and for an explosive second half highlighted by a monster Dark Star/St Stephen/The Eleven jam.  The soundboard source delivers generally clear, full‑bodied audio, though a few reviewers note occasional hiss or mix glitches on Slewfoot and a brief drum wobble at the start.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews (20+) are 4‑5 stars, overwhelmingly praising the musical setlist and standout moments while noting only minor recording flaws; this yields a high combined rating with strong confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and full‑bodied sound, crisp on most tracks.  Some listeners mention hiss, occasional mixing issues on Slewfoot, and a brief drum imbalance in the opening tunes.

standout_songs: Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens relaxed with new‑song country numbers, then pivots into psychedelic intensity via the Dark Star/St Stephen/Eleven sequence before closing with a bittersweet Baby Blue encore.  Reviewers love the contrast of emerging country‑rock and classic ’69 psychedelia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s pedal steel shines on Slewfoot, Dire Wolf, and early Casey Jones; his guitar tone is repeatedly praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work gets special mention during the Dark Star lovelight section and overall solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir (credited as Bobby) takes lead vocals on Dire Wolf and delivers confident performances throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Organ/keyboard texture noted on Baby Blue (possible Tom Constanten), but no detailed commentary beyond that.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming described as flowing and fun; a brief early‑set imbalance is mentioned, plus Bill loses time on Dupree’s Boat Ride.
